codeReview.summary: The round-2 patch fixes the original `readDirSync()` symlink-directory escape from `fix-restricted-fs-shell-contract`, but it still leaves an ACL leak on the same adapter surface: `lstatSync()` now returns null on ancestor escape symlinks, and `VfsAdapter.lstat()` immediately falls back to async `RestrictedFS.lstat()`, which still follows ancestor symlinks out of sandbox. That means the overall RestrictedFS/VfsAdapter contract is still not fully VAL-FS-019-safe, so the original feature is not yet re-verified.

issues.description: `RestrictedFS.lstat()` still does only a lexical `isAllowed()` check and then delegates directly to `this.vfs.lstat(path)`. After `cc45faf`, `lstatSync()` returns null whenever an ancestor symlink is present (`packages/webapp/src/fs/restricted-fs.ts:249-253`), and `VfsAdapter.lstat()` immediately falls back to this async path (`packages/webapp/src/shell/vfs-adapter.ts:227-242`). But LightningFS `CacheFS.lstat()` still follows ancestor symlinks even when the leaf itself is not followed (`node_modules/@isomorphic-git/lightning-fs/src/CacheFS.js:108-125, 224-225`), so a path like `/shared/link-to-other-scoop/secret-file` can still resolve into a sibling scoop and leak metadata through any shell path that uses `fs.lstat()` (for example just-bash `ls` classification/long-listing code in `packages/webapp/src/vendor/just-bash/dist/bundle/browser.js:55-78`). The new tests only cover direct `RestrictedFS.lstatSync()` plus `ls`/`cat` regressions, so this fallback hole remains untested and unfixed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture note now points reviewers at a closed `readDirSync` caveat and misses the remaining live `lstat` fallback gap. Future workers reading `.factory/library/architecture.md` will think the open issue is still `readDirSync`, even though `cc45faf` fixed that path and the surviving ACL hole is in async `RestrictedFS.lstat()`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:85-98 still says the known caveat is `readDirSync` symlink escape; the fix now guards `readDirSync` with `scanPathForSymlinks` in `packages/webapp/src/fs/restricted-fs.ts:202-278` and adds regression tests at `packages/webapp/tests/fs/restricted-fs-sync.test.ts:391-413` and `512-526`.

summary: Fail: `cc45faf` fixes the prior `readDirSync` symlink-escape blocker from `abde8d7`, but a blocking ancestor-symlink escape remains via the `VfsAdapter.lstat()` fallback to async `RestrictedFS.lstat()`. Because that adapter surface is still ACL-unsafe, the original `fix-restricted-fs-shell-contract` feature is not yet fully re-verified.
